title: Learning Transformation Collaboration and Problem Solving through Project Based Learning on the Human Respiratory System at Public High School 1 Teluk Batang
author: Yuda Arianto,
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This research study aimed to investigate the implementation of Project Based Learning (PjBL) on students' problem- 
solving abilities and collaborative skills in the context of learning the respiratory system. The study was conducted at Public High 
School 1 Teluk Batang, West Kalimantan, Indonesia, involving grade XI science students. The research method used in this study 
is a quantitative method using Posttest Only Control Group Designs. The experimental group received instruction using the PjBL 
model, while the control group followed a conventional teaching approach. Data collection methods included observation, 
posttests, and photo documentation. The collected data were analyzed using descriptive and quantitative analysis, including t-tests 
for comparing the experimental and control groups. The results showed that the implementation of PjBL significantly improved 
students' problem-solving abilities and collaborative skills compared to the conventional teaching approach. The PjBL model 
provided students with practical experiences, teamwork opportunities, and real-world problem-solving tasks, enabling them to 
develop critical, creative, communicative, and collaborative skills. The findings of this study contribute to the understanding of 
effective learning methods in the context of evolving educational needs and provide guidance for teachers and policymakers in 
improving teaching practices. The study emphasizes the importance of equipping students with strong problem-solving and 
collaborative abilities to face the challenges of the industrial revolution 5.0.

keywords: Project Based Learning (PjBL), Collaboration, Problem Solving
